title: Cryptography
overview: The science of protecting information so adversaries can’t read or alter it.

study.overview: The science of protecting information so adversaries can’t read or alter it.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.

importantCommands.code:
hashcat -m 1000 -a 0 hash.txt rockyou.txt   # NTLM
john --format=raw-md5 --wordlist=rockyou.txt hash.txt
sha256sum file.iso   # compare with official checksum

openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -in secret.txt -out secret.enc -k mypassword
openssl enc -d -aes-256-cbc -in secret.enc -out secret.txt -k mypassword

openssl genrsa -out private.pem 2048
openssl rsa -in private.pem -pubout -out public.pem
openssl rsautl -encrypt -inkey public.pem -pubin -in secret.txt -out secret.enc
openssl rsautl -decrypt -inkey private.pem -in secret.enc -out secret.txt
